title: eucnide tenella
intro: Eucnide tenella, also known as the Desert Marigold, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The medicinal properties of Eucnide tenella include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been used to treat a variety of ailments. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems. Eucnide tenella can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
intro_study:
I think you meant Euclea natalensis, not eucnide tenella. 

According to "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", Euclea natalensis has antidiabetic properties, among other benefits.
